cards.title: Emotional maturity helps raise happy, healthy children
cards.body: Psychologist Lindsay C Gibson's book 'Adult Children of Emotionally Immature Parents' became an unexpected pandemic bestseller. She now offers advice for parents on raising emotionally healthy children. The focus is on understanding emotional maturity and its impact on family dynamics.

cards.title: How sport shaped Olympic champion's childhood development
cards.body: Paralympic swimmer Ellie Simmonds credits swimming with her personal development and success as a teenager. She moved to Swansea at age 11 to train with the GB squad and competed at 13. Her mother credits the sport with keeping her on a positive life path.

cards.title: Parents powerless during teenage A-level exam years
cards.body: Parents of teenagers taking A-levels often feel helpless supporting their children through challenging subjects. The pressure of holding flashcards for complex subjects like chemistry creates unique parental anxiety. This stage of parenting leaves many feeling genuinely impotent despite their desire to help.

cards.title: Why families need space to grow together
cards.body: Living apart from family members can trigger guilt and sadness but also enable healthy growth. Healthy distance helps children, parents, and partners develop independently while maintaining love and connection. Psychologists explain how space paradoxically strengthens family bonds.

cards.title: What to do when you dislike your teenager
cards.body: Parents sometimes struggle with their child's attitude during difficult teenage years. Experts provide practical strategies to make the teenage phase easier for both parent and child. Understanding underlying causes of teen behavior helps parents respond more effectively.

cards.title: BFRB treatment works even without child's willingness
cards.body: Body-focused repetitive behaviors (BFRBs) like hair pulling and skin picking can be treated before a child wants to stop. Meaningful treatment begins with acceptance rather than waiting for the child's readiness. Understanding the psychology of BFRBs helps parents support effective intervention.

cards.title: ADHD symptoms may look different across cultures
cards.body: ADHD assessments sometimes appear straightforward but miss important cultural context affecting symptom presentation. Life transitions and cultural background significantly influence how ADHD manifests in children. Clinicians must explore broader context beyond surface-level symptom checklists for accurate diagnosis.
